messages.content: i am having 20 years of experience out side canada  as mechanical engineer, how to get engineering practice licence in Canada

To obtain an engineering practice license in Canada, you will need to follow these steps:

1. Obtain a degree in mechanical engineering from a recognized institution. You will need to have a degree that is recognized by the Canadian Engineering Accreditation Board (CEAB) or be assessed by the National Credential Assessment Service (NCAS) to determine whether your degree is equivalent to a Canadian engineering degree.

2. Apply for membership with the engineering regulatory body in the province or territory where you plan to work. Each province and territory has its own regulatory body, and membership requirements may vary.

3. Submit your application for licensure and provide documentation of your work experience. You will need to provide information about your previous work experience and demonstrate that you have met the competency requirements for licensure.

4. Complete any additional requirements. Depending on the province or territory, you may need to complete an ethics exam or additional training before you can receive your engineering license.

5. Pass the Professional Practice Examination (PPE). The PPE is a nationally recognized exam that tests your knowledge of engineering ethics, law, and professional practice. You will need to pass this exam before you can receive your engineering license.

Once you have completed these steps, you will be granted a license to practice engineering in Canada.
can i take PPE test outside canada , meaning before coming to canada

Yes, you can take the Professional Practice Examination (PPE) outside Canada. The PPE is offered by Engineers Canada, and you can take the exam at approved locations outside of Canada. 

However, before you can take the PPE, you will need to apply for licensure with the engineering regulatory body in the province or territory where you plan to work. Each province or territory has its own licensure requirements, so you will need to check with the regulatory body to determine what you need to do.

In addition, you may need to provide documentation of your work experience and demonstrate that you have met the competency requirements for licensure before you can take the PPE. You should consult with the regulatory body to determine what requirements you need to meet before you can take the exam.
can you guide me to start with the first process

Sure, here are the steps you can follow to start the process of obtaining an engineering practice license in Canada:

1. Determine which province or territory you plan to work in. Each province and territory has its own regulatory body, so you will need to decide where you plan to work in order to determine which regulatory body you will need to apply to.

2. Research the requirements for licensure with the engineering regulatory body in the province or territory where you plan to work. You will need to determine what education and experience requirements you need to meet, as well as any other requirements, such as language proficiency.

3. Obtain an assessment of your degree from the National Credential Assessment Service (NCAS) if necessary. If your degree is not recognized by the Canadian Engineering Accreditation Board (CEAB), you will need to have it assessed to determine whether it is equivalent to a Canadian engineering degree.

4. Apply for membership with the engineering regulatory body in the province or territory where you plan to work. You will need to submit an application, pay any fees, and provide documentation of your education and work experience.

5. Complete any additional requirements, such as an ethics exam or additional training.

6. Prepare for and take the Professional Practice Examination (PPE), if required. The PPE is a nationally recognized exam that tests your knowledge of engineering ethics, law, and professional practice.

7. Once you have met all the requirements, you will be granted a license to practice engineering in Canada.

Please note that these steps are general guidelines, and the specific process may vary depending on the province or territory where you plan to work. It is important to research the requirements of the engineering regulatory body in your specific province or territory for more detailed information.
My engineering assessment done from WES canada , Is WES not valid

WES (World Education Services) is a credential evaluation service that is commonly used in Canada. If you had your engineering degree assessed by WES Canada, it means that WES has evaluated your credentials to determine how they compare to Canadian standards. 

This assessment can be a useful tool when you are applying for licensure with the engineering regulatory body in Canada. However, it is important to note that each province or territory has its own licensure requirements and may have its own process for evaluating foreign credentials. 

Therefore, in addition to the WES assessment, you will typically need to provide documentation of your education and work experience to the engineering regulatory body in the province or territory where you plan to work. The regulatory body will then evaluate your credentials and determine whether they meet their standards for licensure.

In short, while the WES assessment can be a helpful tool, it is typically not the only requirement for licensure with the engineering regulatory body in Canada. Therefore, you should consult with the regulatory body in your province or territory to determine what additional requirements you need to meet in order to be eligible for licensure.
there is any consultant whom i can pay for preparing all my documents

Yes, there are consultant services available that can help you prepare your documents for licensure as an engineer in Canada. These services can assist you with evaluating your credentials, preparing your application, and providing guidance on the licensure process.

However, it is important to be cautious when choosing a consultant, as there have been cases of fraudulent practices in the industry. You should do your research and choose a reputable consultant or firm that has experience working with the specific licensing requirements in the province or territory where you plan to work. 

The regulatory body in your province or territory may have a list of approved consultants or resources that you can consult to help you through the process. Additionally, you can also check with professional engineering associations in Canada, such as Engineers Canada, for recommendations on where to find reputable consultant services.

Keep in mind that the use of a consultant is entirely optional and there are no guarantees that using their services will increase your chances of success in obtaining licensure. Ultimately, the responsibility for meeting the requirements of licensure rests with the applicant.
among this four province in canada, which one has more construction happening

The construction industry in Canada is active in multiple provinces, and each province has various areas with different levels of construction activity. However, here is some information on the construction industry in four of Canada's most populous provinces:

1. Ontario - Ontario is Canada's most populous province and has a large construction industry. It is home to Toronto, which is the largest city in Canada and has a significant amount of construction activity, particularly in residential and commercial buildings.

2. Quebec - Quebec is the second most populous province in Canada, with Montreal being the largest city. Like Ontario, Quebec has a large construction industry, with activities ranging from residential and commercial buildings to infrastructure projects such as roads and bridges.

3. British Columbia - British Columbia is the third most populous province in Canada and has a strong construction industry. Vancouver is the largest city in British Columbia and has a significant amount of construction activity, particularly in residential and commercial buildings.

4. Alberta - Alberta is the fourth most populous province in Canada and has a thriving construction industry due to a rapidly growing population and strong economy. Calgary and Edmonton are the largest cities in Alberta and have significant construction activity in commercial, residential, and infrastructure projects.

Please note that this is just a general overview, and construction activity can vary greatly depending on the specific city or region within each province.
among the above four province , which one is having the most extreme climate ?

Among the four provinces of Ontario, Quebec, British Columbia, and Alberta, Alberta is known to have the most extreme climate. Alberta is located between the Rocky Mountains to the west and the prairies to the east, which creates a range of weather patterns and climate conditions.

In the winter, temperatures in Alberta can drop to extreme lows, with some areas experiencing temperatures as low as -40°C (-40°F). The province also experiences significant snowfall in the winter months, particularly in the mountainous regions.

In the summer, temperatures in Alberta can reach high temperatures, with some areas experiencing temperatures as high as 30°C (86°F) to 35°C (95°F). However, the province's location also means that it can experience a wide range of weather conditions throughout the year, including wind, rain, and thunderstorms.

Overall, Alberta's location between the Rocky Mountains and the prairies means that it experiences a range of weather patterns and climate conditions, making it one of the most extreme climate regions among the four provinces listed.
Ontario, Britich columbia ,qubec - which one is having extreme climate ?

Among the three provinces of Ontario, British Columbia, and Quebec, Quebec is known to have the most extreme climate. Quebec is located in Eastern Canada and is known for its long, cold winters and hot, humid summers.

In the winter, Quebec can experience extreme cold temperatures, with some areas experiencing temperatures as low as -35°C (-31°F) to -40°C (-40°F). The province also experiences significant snowfall, which can cause transportation disruptions and other challenges.

In the summer, temperatures in Quebec can reach high temperatures, with some areas experiencing temperatures as high as 30°C (86°F) to 35°C (95°F). The province is also known for its high humidity levels, which can make the summer weather conditions feel particularly uncomfortable.

Ontario and British Columbia also have their own climate extremes, albeit different from Quebec. Ontario can experience significant fluctuations in temperature and precipitation throughout the year, with hot, humid summers and cold, snowy winters. British Columbia has a mild, rainy climate on the coast, but its interior regions can have colder winter temperatures and significant snowfall.

Overall, Quebec is known for its more extreme climate conditions among the three provinces of Ontario, British Columbia, and Quebec.
Ontorio and british columbia which one is having extreme climate ?

Between Ontario and British Columbia, Ontario is more likely to experience extreme climate conditions. Ontario is located in Central Canada and has a humid continental climate that can result in a range of weather conditions.

In the winter, temperatures in Ontario can drop to extreme lows, with some areas experiencing temperatures as low as -40°C (-40°F). The province also experiences significant snowfall in the winter months, particularly in the areas near the Great Lakes.

In the summer, temperatures in Ontario can reach high temperatures, with some areas experiencing temperatures as high as 30°C (86°F) to 35°C (95°F). However, the province's location also means that it can experience a wide range of weather conditions throughout the year, including thunderstorms, tornadoes, and severe winter storms.

British Columbia, on the other hand, has a milder climate compared to Ontario, with an oceanic climate on the coast and a semi-arid climate in the interior. While it can experience cold temperatures and snowfall in certain areas during the winter season, the coastal region receives a lot of precipitation and relatively moderated temperatures year-round.

Overall, Ontario is more likely to experience extreme climate conditions among the two provinces mentioned due to its geographic location and humid continental climate.
among the above four province which one is more immigration friendly

All four Canadian provinces of Ontario, Quebec, British Columbia, and Alberta are immigration-friendly, as they have multiple programs dedicated to welcoming and facilitating the settlement of newcomers into their communities. However, here is some information on each province's specific immigration programs:

1. Ontario - Ontario has multiple ways for immigrants to apply for provincial nomination, express entry, and other immigration streams. Some of these include the Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP), the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker Stream, the Masters Graduate Stream, and the PhD Graduate Stream.

2. Quebec - Quebec has its unique immigration programs, including the Quebec Skilled Worker Program, Quebec Experience Program, and Quebec Entrepreneur Program. It also has a dedicated immigration portal, Arrima, which helps connect potential immigrants to job opportunities in the province.

3. British Columbia - British Columbia has the British Columbia Provincial Nominee Program (BCPNP), which offers multiple pathways for immigration, including the Skills Immigration stream, the Express Entry BC stream, and the Entrepreneur Immigration stream.

4. Alberta - Alberta has a number of programs designed to encourage and facilitate immigration, including the Alberta Immigrant Nominee Program (AINP), which offers pathways for skilled workers and immigrants looking to invest in businesses in the province.

Overall, all four provinces have a range of programs, policies, and initiatives designed to attract, retain, and support immigrants, making them all immigration-friendly. However, specific programs and streams may vary depending on province's policies and goals.
above four province which one is more ecnomically strong

Each of the four provinces of Ontario, Quebec, British Columbia, and Alberta has a strong and diverse economy, with different economic sectors contributing to their overall economic growth. Here is some information on each province's economy:

1. Ontario - Ontario has the largest economy in Canada and is home to several key industries such as manufacturing, finance, and technology. The province's economy is also diversified, with significant agricultural production and a growing service sector.

2. Quebec - Like Ontario, Quebec has a diverse economy, with key industries such as manufacturing, aerospace, and technology. The province is also known for its significant mineral and timber resources and has a growing renewable energy sector.

3. British Columbia - British Columbia has a strong and diverse economy, with key industries such as forestry, mining, and technology. The province is also known for its significant agricultural production, and its coastal location has made it a hub for international trade.

4. Alberta - Alberta is known for its natural resources, particularly oil and gas. The province's economy also includes significant agricultural and forestry production, as well as a growing technology sector.

Overall, all four provinces have a strong and diverse economy, with different sectors contributing to their overall economic growth. However, Ontario has the largest economy in Canada, while Quebec, British Columbia, and Alberta also have unique and thriving industries that are contributing to their economic growth and strength.
among the above province which one is more easy to get PE engineering licence

The requirements for obtaining a Professional Engineer (PE) license may vary depending on the province or territory where you plan to work. Therefore, it is difficult to determine which province would be the easiest to obtain a PE engineering license without additional information.

That being said, each province and territory in Canada generally requires that applicants have a degree in engineering from an accredited institution, completion of a national licensing exam such as the Professional Practice Examination (PPE), and documentation of relevant work experience.

Some provinces may also require additional steps such as professional development courses, technical exams, and language proficiency tests. It's important to note that the licensing requirements in each province can change over time.

Overall, the best way to determine the specific requirements and steps to obtain a PE engineering license in any province is to visit the website of the professional engineering regulator in that province. The regulatory body will provide detailed information on the licensure process, including the requirements and steps involved, as well as any relevant resources or contacts to help you through the process.
recruitment consultancy fees for providing work visa for canada is 10,000 to 15000 canadian dollar , is this high as per your survey ?

As an AI language model, I am not able to conduct surveys. However, I can provide you some general information on recruitment consultancy fees for providing work visas in Canada.

Recruitment consultancy fees for providing work visas in Canada can vary depending on several factors, including the type of job, the level of experience required, and the location of the job. Generally, the fees can range from a few hundred to several thousand Canadian dollars.

In some cases, a recruitment consultancy may charge a higher fee for services such as job placement or additional services such as visa application assistance, immigration advice, and relocation support.

It's important to research and compare the fees charged by different recruitment consultancies, as well as their reputation and the services they offer. Candidates may also want to consider alternative paths to work visas, such as applying for a visa through the Canadian government or contacting employers directly.

Overall, it may be difficult to determine whether the fees charged by a recruitment consultancy are high or not without more information on the specific services and level of support provided. Therefore, it's essential to research and compare different options before making a decision.
can you please provide an assumption

Based on general information available online, a recruitment consultancy fee of CAD 10,000 to CAD 15,000 for work visas in Canada may be considered high. However, it's important to note that the fees charged by recruitment consultancies can vary depending on various factors. 

The high end of the range is significantly higher than most of the typical fees for work visa services. According to Canadian immigration law, it's illegal for a consultant to charge for finding a job in Canada. They can only charge you for any counseling or consulting services provided in relation to your job. 

Therefore, it's important to research and compare fees charged by different recruitment consultancies to ensure you are getting a fair price for the services and level of support provided. It's also important to ensure that the consultancy is reputable, licensed, and follows the laws and regulations in place to protect workers and job seekers in Canada.
